_meta.source: crystals.com safety guide + gemporia/consciousitems/cosmopolitan cleansing methods (SERP 2026-06-27)
_meta.for: Cleansing Timer工具(T4) + 净化文章
_meta.note: 7 methods 时长+safety 规则;universal_safe 4 法全石安全;合规:tradition/practical 双视角,no scientific evidence

moonlight.name: Moonlight
moonlight.tradition: Place under the full moon to reset a stone's symbolic energy; the full moon is considered the most potent time.
moonlight.practical: Moonlight is reflected sunlight at roughly 1/400,000 the intensity — no UV risk, no damage to any mineral.
steps: Place crystals on a windowsill or covered porch at sunset
Leave overnight (8+ hours)
Bring in before sunrise to avoid dew or direct sun

selenite.name: Selenite Plate / Bowl
selenite.tradition: Selenite is widely considered self-cleansing and is said to cleanse stones placed on it.
selenite.practical: No chemical interaction — completely harmless. Handle gently, as selenite itself is soft (Mohs 2).
steps: Place crystals on a selenite plate, bowl, or bar
Leave 6+ hours or overnight
Avoid dragging hard stones across the selenite surface

sound.name: Sound (Singing Bowl / Bell)
sound.tradition: Sustained sound vibrations are believed to clear stagnant energy around crystals.
sound.practical: Sound waves are far too low-energy to affect any mineral's structure — harmless to every stone.
steps: Play a singing bowl, bell, or tuning fork near the crystals
Continue for 5–10 minutes
Works for your whole collection at once

smoke.name: Smoke (Sage / Palo Santo)
smoke.tradition: Passing crystals through smoke is believed to clear stored energy; rooted in many indigenous practices.
smoke.practical: Smoke will not damage stones. Prolonged exposure may leave faint residue — wipe with a dry cloth.
steps: Light sage, palo santo, or cedar
Pass each crystal through the smoke for 30–60 seconds
Ensure ventilation and use fire safely

water.name: Running Water
water.tradition: Holding a crystal under running water is believed to wash away stored negativity.
water.practical: Effective physical cleaning for hard, non-porous stones; dissolves or damages soft or water-reactive minerals.
steps: Rinse under cool (not hot) running water
About 1 minute, gently moving fingers over the stone
Dry immediately with a soft cloth

earth.name: Earth Burial
earth.tradition: Burying crystals returns them to the earth to absorb and neutralize stored energy.
earth.practical: Soil moisture and organic acids can stain or etch soft, porous stones — always wrap in cloth first.
steps: Wrap the stone in a natural cloth
Bury in soil (garden or houseplant pot)
Leave about 24 hours
Brush off soil gently when done

salt.name: Salt (Dry)
salt.tradition: Salt is one of the oldest purification symbols, believed to draw out and neutralize negative energy.
salt.practical: The most damaging method — abrasive, corrodes metals, etches soft stones. Saltwater is not recommended for any specimen-quality crystal.
steps: Bury in dry sea salt or Himalayan salt
Only Mohs 7+ stones, a few hours maximum
Discard the salt afterward (do not reuse)

frequency.physical: Monthly wipe-down with a dry or slightly damp microfiber cloth (like mineral-museum care).
frequency.ritual: Whenever it feels right — a new crystal, after heavy use, on the full moon, or at the start of a new season.

accessories.name: Selenite Charging Plate
accessories.desc: Universally safe cleansing + a beautiful display base.

accessories.name: White Sage Smudge Stick
accessories.desc: Smoke cleansing that works for every stone.

accessories.name: Tibetan Singing Bowl
accessories.desc: Sound cleansing for your whole collection at once.
